title: Uniface Desktop Applications
text: Uniface Desktop Applications Uniface desktop applications are client/server applications, in which an application shell and form components run locally. Usually, service and report components run on a remote server. Most desktop applications run on Windows, but they can also run as character-based applications on any supported platform. The user interface of desktop applications consists of: An application shell, which initializes the application and defines the window in which it runs (or the layout used in character-based applications). For more information, see Application Shells for Desktop Applications . Form components that provide an interactive user interface in which users can display and edit data. Forms support a rich set of widgets and layout objects, such as area frames and split bars, for building an attractive and flexible user interface. For more information, see Forms . Global objects, such as menus and panels for use in forms and application shells, messages for user feedback, and drag-and-drop formats. For more information, see Global Objects . Report components, which retrieve data and print reports. Headers, footers, and break frames provide print-specific functionality. For more information, see Reports . Other application processing can be provided by service components that are activated by other components. To start the application, the user runs the application shell using the Uniface executable ( uniface.exe ). The application shell activates the first form and passes control to it. Related Links Uniface Applications Uniface applications are typically mission-critical enterprise applications that use and maintain data stored in one or more databases, files, or other data resources. They may integrate with all kinds of non-Uniface applications, and they can be deployed on a wide variety of platforms. How a Desktop Application Works A Uniface desktop application is a traditional client/server application which can run on a local machine with access to remote functionality when needed.
